title: Stabilize status tests under color
goal: Disable ANSI colors globally in the vitest test setup (e.g. via NO_COLOR before picocolors loads) so npm test is deterministic regardless of the ambient CI env var, fixing the two status.test.ts assertions that break on GitHub Actions where picocolors auto-enables color.

steps.title: Add NO_COLOR to vitest.config.ts test.env
steps.detail: Set test: { env: { NO_COLOR: '1' } } in vitest.config.ts with a short comment explaining that GitHub Actions CI=true makes picocolors enable ANSI, which splits literal substrings in status assertions.

steps.title: Verify the fix under simulated CI
steps.detail: Run CI=1 npm test; confirm the two previously failing status.test.ts tests pass and the whole suite is green, proving test.env is applied before picocolors evaluates color support at import time.

steps.title: Fallback to setupFiles only if test.env is too late
steps.detail: If step 2 still shows the tests red (env applied after picocolors import), add a setupFiles entry that sets process.env.NO_COLOR before module imports, and re-run. Skip entirely if step 2 already passes.

steps.title: Confirm no regressions: statusline + plain run + build
steps.detail: Verify src/statusline/render.test.ts (hardcoded ANSI) stays green under CI=1 NO_COLOR=1, that plain npm test (no CI env) is still green, and that npm run typecheck and npm run build pass.

autoCommit.subject: Phase 182: Stabilize status tests under color
